evidence: Docstring: 'The Beal Conjecture: if ... A^x + B^y = C^z then A, B, C have a common divisor', the exact statement of the referenced Wikipedia Beal conjecture article.

evidence: Docstring 'The Beck–Fiala theorem' states the 2t-1 discrepancy bound of Beck and Fiala (1981), the statement named by the Wikidata item.
verify_note: BeckFiala.beck_fiala_theorem exists (@[category research solved]); states the 2t-1 degree-t discrepancy bound, exactly the statement Q17002928 denotes.

evidence: Module docstring frames the result as 'Discrepancy of bounded-degree set systems' minimizing the discrepancy max_i |sum_{j in S_i} chi(j)|.
verify_note: The statement is a discrepancy bound max_i |sum chi| <= 2t-1; discrepancy is the central quantity, load-bearing; Q1228843 live.

evidence: Docstring 'The Beck–Fiala conjecture' is the O(sqrt t) strengthening described on the referenced Beck–Fiala theorem Wikipedia page; Wikidata has no separate item for the conjecture.
verify_note: Correctly filed as mentions, not formalizes: the conjecture (O(sqrt t)) is a distinct strengthening covered on the Beck-Fiala theorem page and Wikidata has no separate item; the theorem concept is load-bearing.

evidence: The conjecture asserts the discrepancy of a degree-t set system is O(sqrt t), a central question of combinatorial discrepancy theory per the module docstring.
verify_note: BeckFiala.beck_fiala_conjecture exists (@[category research open]); asserts a discrepancy bound, core discrepancy-theory question; load-bearing mention.

verify_note: Brocard.brocard_conjecture exists (@[category research open]); at least 4 primes between p_n^2 and p_{n+1}^2, exactly the statement Q509160 denotes.
